condition_A_validity: COMPATIBLE_WITH_MODEL_RESPONSE_SCHEMA; short definitions and review-status wording only; execution blocked pending rights, private packets, account-verified model, runtime settings, and final authorization
condition_B_validity: COMPATIBLE_WITH_MODEL_RESPONSE_SCHEMA; concise operational rules only; no full manual content; execution blocked pending rights, private packets, account-verified model, runtime settings, and final authorization
difference_assessment: A, B, and C share the same system prompt, source-packet envelope, model response schema, non-truth-verdict boundary, evidence-ID requirement, alternative handling, no-browsing/no-tools rule, and session isolation. They differ only in the declared condition payload.
manipulation_name: shared structured annotation baseline with increasing levels of interpretive guidance
condition_A_increment: category names, short definitions, and review-status wording
condition_B_increment: concise operational sequence, candidate-count dominance rules, cue/context positive rules, and review-linkage rule
condition_C_increment: full authoritative JSON manual content, full pairwise guidance, complete counterfactual suite, governance details, and manual worked examples
